section_title: INITIAL MARKET WATCH AND PMI NEWS EXPECTATION
content:
Just a quick little video. This is the area I was watching this morning. We were waiting for the PMI number
to come out at 9:45. I was expecting it to likely whip up into that and then look for a sell-off. I tweeted about
this this morning as a potential scenario. We were waiting for 9:45 news, not just simply waiting for the 9:30
opening.

section_title: IDENTIFYING KEY PRICE LEVELS AND RANGE CONFINEMENT
content:
This old high here is the highest one in the range from today's opening. Looking back, this is the first one
you'd go to that would be higher than this one. Before we get this volume imbalance, we are essentially
stuck inside this range, waiting for it to come out of that. We can obviously trade inside this range, but it
takes a little bit more patience.

section_title: HOURLY CHART ANALYSIS AND LIQUIDITY SWEEPS
content:
We can drop down into the 60-minute chart (one-hour chart). You can see how we did not get up into that
employment balance on the daily chart. We swept the buy-side liquidity twice, once, twice, and then broke
down. Everybody got the sell-side liquidity, and on the 30-minute, whatever you got. We have this big run
here.

section_title: 30-MINUTE CHART IMBALANCE AND CONFIRMATION WAITING
content:
If we drop down into a 30-minute chart, I use the 30-minute chart because you can clearly see that
imbalance right there. The failure to get up above this high here and run for that is what we were waiting for
as confirmation one way or the other, if it was going to run there. Not in a hurry to take a trade.

section_title: FAIR VALUE GAP IDENTIFICATION AND PRICE DRAWDOWN EXPECTATION
content:
It's the first trading day of the year; everybody's going to be wanting to get in there and do something right
away. We have a fair value gap there, and you can see all those tweets were mentioned before the fact. I
mentioned that we would draw down likely into this area here, so let's go into a 15-minute.

section_title: 15-MINUTE FAIR VALUE GAP AND LOWER PRICE ACTION
content:
A very clear 15-minute time frame fair value gap was observed. There was a beautiful return into it,
repricing, and then price moved lower down to a nice start of 3014 and a half. Now to the five-minute chart.
ONE
- MINUTE CHART:

section_title: BUYSIDE SWEEP AND MARKET STRUCTURE SHIFT
content:
The market trades above where the buy side is, a nice round number at 3900, then breaks lower. We're
going to look at this low on a one-minute chart. On the one-minute chart, you can see we had a run up into
where buy-side is. The market then breaks down and takes out a swing low.

section_title: INSTITUTIONAL OVERFLOW ENTRY DRILL AND MARKET STRUCTURE CHANGE
content:
Why do I like this low? It may be something like this one, not so much. You could have used this one here
where we went below it right on that candle. Then we traded right up inside this imbalance there. That's
more or less an institutional overflow entry drill. It breaks lower, really changes the market structure here
and over here. So this is going to be a little bit more of a quality type setup.

section_title: BEARISH ICT BREAKER AND MEAN THRESHOLD
content:
To have a fair value gap in here, as it traded up into that, that's an expectation of lower prices because this
is also a breaker, a bearish ICT breaker. Inside this last down-close candle, it's returning back up. Notice
the bodies of the candle; they're respecting half of that range from the candle's high and low. That right
there is mean threshold. Mean threshold is half of an order block.

section_title: IMBALANCE REPAIR AND BEARISH ORDER BLOCK CONFIRMATION
content:
The market breaks lower, creating another imbalance or repair of a gap from this candle's high and low,
including the shaded area. We have up-close candles, and once broken through, it signifies a bearish order
block. There are multiple confirmations for a short seller:
- Bearish order block upon breaking through up-close candles.
- Institutional order flow entry drill.
- Fair value gap.
These indications support short selling on this candle as price trades up into it and then trades lower,
leaving an open range, which is identified as a Breakaway Gap.

section_title: SHORT ENTRY AND PRICE ACCELERATION INTO FAIR VALUE GAPS
content:
The market trades lower into our fair value gap on the 15-minute time frame. It consolidates, I go short one
more time, and it accelerates down into the 30-minute fair value gap here. As we went down below it, a limit
order was filled. Then we retraced all the way back up to a perfect return to the 15-minute fair value gap.
Look at the precision there.

section_title: ALGORITHMIC PRECISION VS. CLASSIC SUPPORT/RESISTANCE
content:
Now go back and look over here, do you see any kind of classic support/resistance that would justify that
perfect precision right there? No, but the algorithm does and sees the fair value gap that I've outlined on
the 15-minute time frame. That's real support and resistance, folks. The market trades lower down into the
low end of our 30-minute fair value gap, beautiful delivery.

section_title: IMPORTANCE OF PERSONAL CHART MARKING FOR LEARNING
content:
I more or less told you all to screenshot it on your own chart, not just simply take my charts, because that's
a lazy person's approach to doing it. You won't learn that way. You want to get in here and get comfortable
marking your charts up. It takes a little bit of effort, takes time, and it's important for you to be able to do it. If
you don't do it, if you don't like doing it, I promise you trading won't be a successful endeavor for you. You
have to be involved, you have to do it yourself, and it requires a lot of effort on the part of the students.
DEFINITIONS:

section_title: MEAN THRESHOLD AND CONSEQUENT ENCROACHMENT
content:
The market hangs around, working inside. Key definitions:
- Consequent Encroachment: The middle of an imbalance (or a gap/inefficiency like a fair value gap, buy
- side imbalance, sell-side efficiency, sell-side imbalance, buy-side efficiency).
- Mean Threshold: The middle of an order block.
These concepts are important for future mentorship.

section_title: PRICE ACTION TOWARDS TURNING POINT AND CONSOLIDATION
content:
But it goes halfway up into that and then clears the buy side. The sell side is below here. Attack it runs
down to a really beautiful turning point at 3014 and a half. And then now we've returned back up into that
30-minute fair value gap and are now consolidating in and around and working the top end of that 30
- minute fair value gap.

section_title: INTRADAY SCALPING VS. DAILY NARRATIVE
content:
So we'll be looking to see an eventual breakout. Not that I'm a breakout trader, but we want to see a
breakout of that daily range before my interest has really peaked on any sustainable price run for analysis
purposes. Otherwise, in layman's terms, in easy language, we have to be an intraday scalper until there's a
higher time frame daily narrative at work. So that way, everything we look for is on a small time frame and
not expecting big monster moves.

section_title: SHORT ENTRIES AND JUDAS SWING EXPECTATION
content:
All right, so we're watching price this morning, the first trading day of the year. I went short on two fair value
gaps. One at the top of the orange fair value gap at 38.60, looking for continuation down into the levels I
shared on Twitter. Initially, I thought that we would get 39.20 as a run-up, an aggressive run as a Judas
swing, like a fake rally aggressive during the PMI numbers at 9:45. The market was not having that, so
that's why I said we have to wait and see what the market does.

section_title: MARKET STRUCTURE SHIFT AND SHORT ENTRY CONFIRMATIONS
content:
Price failed to run above the 39.05 level. I waited for a shift in market structure just below 38.72-38.73,
followed by a return into a fair value gap. Entries observed on the one-minute chart included:
- Short entry after a breakdown and re-entry into a fair value gap.
- Short entry into a bearish order block.
- Third entry at the top of the orange fair value gap at 38.60, as it traded up into that, looking for heaviness
(lower prices) towards the 38.39 and a quarter level.

section_title: TRADE MANAGEMENT AND EXPECTATION OF LOWER PRICES
content:
So I took one partial, leaving two contracts on, and I want to get below that 38.42.50 level. That's where
sell side is, you can see that noted on the right-hand side on a 30-minute chart. Why am I using a 30
- minute chart? I want to see price remain below that fair value gap here on the left-hand side. If it goes
above that, then I'll probably want to close the trades, I'm thinking, but I want to see it remain heavy. Heavy
means I want to see it go lower and have difficulty rallying higher.

section_title: FINAL TARGETS AND LOW-HANGING FRUIT OBJECTIVE
content:
And if we trade down below 38.42.50, I will take one off. But rejecting that 38 I'm sorry, 39.20 level like I
was expecting, that daily volume imbalance up there (right-hand side chart, the upper orange level), that to
me was a likelihood of a draw on the news driver. I'll take another partial off here, and we'll see if it wants to
go down and take that. I mean, a low-hanging fruit objective now. And we'll see if we get that boom.
